info.title: Agentic Developer Hub API
info.description:
Backend API for the Agentic Developer Hub ecosystem.

Consumers: temporal.today (web app), admin.temporal.today (admin
dashboard), native iOS/macOS/Android/Windows clients, and the
macOS/Windows daemons.

## Authentication

Protected endpoints require a JWT bearer token in the
Authorization header. Obtain a token via one of:

- **Provider-agnostic OAuth** — `GET /api/auth/providers` to
  discover providers, then `POST /api/auth/complete` (direct)
  or `GET /api/auth/start` → callback → `POST /api/auth/exchange`
  (redirect flow). Supports OAuth2 code, access-token, and
  OIDC id-token auth types.
- **Google OAuth (legacy)** — `POST /api/auth/google` with a
  Google ID token.
- **Email / password** — `POST /api/auth/register` or
  `POST /api/auth/login`.

## Admin endpoints

Routes under `/api/admin/` require the caller to hold the
`admin` capability. They manage OAuth clients, user roles,
personas, persona services, integration configs, and messaging.

## WebSocket

Real-time events (replies, notifications, typing, presence, DMs)
flow over a multiplexed WebSocket at `ws://host/ws/discussions`.
The first message must be the JWT; subsequent messages are JSON
envelopes — see the shared/model package for message shapes.

## Webhooks

Inbound webhook receivers (e.g. Google Calendar push
notifications) live under `/api/webhooks/`. These are called by
external services, not by API consumers.

get.description: Liveness check
Liveness check (alias)
Readiness check including database connectivity
API version, minimum client version, and current DB schema version

post.description: Sign in with a Google ID token. Verifies with Google, creates user on first sign-in, returns Temporal JWT + refresh token.
Create a new account with email + password
Sign in with email + password

401.description: Invalid credentials or password not set

post.description: Exchange a refresh token for a new JWT + rotated refresh token. Reads the refresh token from the `refresh_token` cookie; falls back to the request body for clients that haven't migrated.
Revoke a refresh token (sign out). Reads the token from the `refresh_token` cookie; falls back to the request body. Clears the cookie on the response.

get.description: Return the authenticated user (with capabilities)

post.description: Revoke every refresh token owned by the caller (sign out of all sessions)
Add password authentication to an existing account (e.g. Google-only)
Change the caller's password. Requires the current password; revokes all refresh tokens on success.

409.description: No password is set on this account — use /auth/link-password

get.description: List OAuth providers allowed for a given client

post.description: Complete an OAuth sign-in (any provider, any auth type). Internal clients omit the Authorization header; external clients must present a Bearer app token.

get.description: Initiate a redirect-based OAuth flow (oauth2_code providers only). Redirects the browser to the provider's authorize URL.

parameters.name: return
parameters.description: URL to redirect back to after auth

get.description: OAuth provider callback. Exchanges the provider's authorization code for a single-use exchange code and redirects back to the client's return URL with the code in the fragment.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Authorization code from the OAuth provider

302.description: Redirect to return URL with exchange code in fragment

post.description: Exchange a single-use code (from the callback redirect) for a JWT and refresh token
Receive Google Calendar push notifications. Google sends channel ID and resource state in headers.

parameters.description: Pull ops with seq > this value (default 0)

post.description: Apply a single document operation (insert/delete/move/etc.) to the document op log

get.description: Get a fresh presigned download URL for an attachment

delete.description: Delete a named version (does not affect op log)

get.description: Load the document state at a specific version

post.description: Restore a version by writing inverse ops as a single undo group

Bidirectional sync — clients send local edits and new records; server returns conflicts and merged state. Used by Apple, Android, and Windows clients to sync content/project records via LWW + edit operations (see ADR-003).

Connect a provider. Body is a polymorphic ConnectRequest discriminated by 'type' field (oauth | oauth_instance | plaid_link | api_key | app_password).
Trigger an immediate sync of an integration connection

get.description: Query events for the current user, newest first. Filters: type, since (ISO-8601), limit (1-1000).

post.description: Append an event to the per-user append-only log.

get.description: List all queues for the current user, sorted by name.

post.description: Append a message to the named queue. The queue is created on first use.
Claim the oldest pending message and flip it to in_flight. Returns 204 if the queue is empty.
Acknowledge an in_flight message, removing it from delivery.
Negative-acknowledge an in_flight message, returning it to pending for re-delivery.

get.description: List all keywords for the current user, sorted by name.

post.description: Tag a target with the named keyword. The keyword is created on first use. Idempotent.

delete.description: Remove a keyword→target link. Returns 404 if the keyword or link does not exist.

get.description: List all targets tagged with the named keyword, in insertion order.
List all secrets for the current user (metadata only — ciphertext omitted).
Get a single secret by name. Returns the opaque base64 ciphertext envelope.

put.description: Upsert a secret. Body carries a base64-encoded ciphertext envelope; the server validates only its byte length and never inspects the contents. Response echoes metadata (no ciphertext) since the client already has it.
